sqlalchemy.description: SQLAlchemy is the Database Toolkit for Python, providing a comprehensive suite of tools for working with databases including an Object Relational Mapper (ORM) and Core expression language for SQL operations.

relational_database.description: SQLAlchemy ORM declarative models and relational database operations.

db_data_model.description: Base class for SQLAlchemy declarative models.

db_sql.description: SQLAlchemy insert construction and Session persistence calls.
db_sql.notes: Validator must establish local SQLAlchemy provenance before confirming this low-confidence candidate. For receiver calls, trace the receiver (including attributes such as self.session) to sqlalchemy.orm.Session or sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio.AsyncSession and reject unrelated objects exposing generic execute, add, or add_all methods. For execute calls, inspect the statement and surrounding flow to confirm a relational database write; reject read-only select/query execution and non-SQLAlchemy calls. For bare calls whose name contains insert, confirm the imported or aliased symbol resolves to a supported SQLAlchemy insert path.
